instructions:
Cleanup cohort 1 of 5 from the 0087 FENCE_RE-fix triage. Mechanical rename: the old `Amity`-prefix root-export style is gone from the current TS SDK surface. Replace the old import names with the current namespace names. ~15 drift pairs across ~12 files.

## Symbols to rename

| Old (drift) | New (current surface) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `AmityUserRepository` | `UserRepository` | namespace |
| `AmityNotificationTrayRepository` | `notificationTray` | namespace, also case change |
| `AmityClient` | `Client` | namespace |
| `AmityPost` | (verify â€” likely no direct equivalent; may be `Amity.Post` type ref) | type â€” investigate |

## Steps

1. Run `python3 .docs-ops/ci/check-drift.py --json-out /tmp/drift-before-0088a.json` to capture the current drift baseline.
2. For each symbol above:
   - Grep the docs (`social-plus-sdk/`, `uikit/`, `use-cases/`) for the old name in import statements specifically (`import { AmityX }` or `import AmityX from`).
   - Replace with the new name. For `AmityPost`: check if the snippet uses it as a TYPE (Amity.Post lives in the global namespace) or as a runtime value. If a type-only usage, change to `Amity.Post` (TS namespace ref). If a runtime usage, investigate per-case.
   - Update any DOWNSTREAM usages in the same code block (e.g. `AmityUserRepository.getUser` â†’ `UserRepository.getUser`).
3. Re-run the gate. Confirm at least 15 pairs cleaned up (target: ~15 from this cohort, but other cohorts may overlap if shared files).

## Out of scope

- Symbols in other cohorts (0088bâ€“e).
- Non-import drift items (member-method renames) â€” those are 0088c.
- Skip markers â€” none needed here per the triage classification.

## Success criteria

- Drift count drops by at least 12 pairs vs the baseline captured in step 1.
- No new (file, ref) pairs introduced.
- All `import AmityUserRepository`, `import AmityClient`, `import AmityNotificationTrayRepository` references replaced.

Report in `notes`: per-symbol count of files changed, drift count before/after.
